prompt: The serialized object graph shares an object but deserialization creates two copies. What is the bug?
choices: Object does not implement Serializable and cannot be written to an ObjectOutputStream
Writing shared to two separate ObjectOutputStream instances loses the shared-object identity; each stream independently serializes the object, so deserialization produces two unrelated copies
ByteArrayOutputStream cannot hold two serialized objects simultaneously
Shared references are automatically maintained across separate ObjectOutputStream instances
explanation: ObjectOutputStream tracks shared references within a single stream using an internal handle table. Writing the same object to two separate streams loses that relationship. To preserve shared identity, write both references through the same ObjectOutputStream.
